content.text:
<user_query>
Run these checks in repo C:/Users/andre/.cursor/worktrees/tpn/cyv and return exact outputs:
1) gh pr view 192 --json number,title,state,mergeStateStatus,reviewDecision,headRefName,baseRefName,isDraft,url
2) gh pr diff 192 --name-only
3) git fetch origin --prune
4) git branch -r --contains a91f27d0
5) git branch -r --contains c8a9b65c
6) az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-api --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
7) az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-api-dev --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
8) az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-web --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
9) az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-web-dev --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
10) powershell Invoke-WebRequest status for:
   - https://biqc-api.azurewebsites.net/api/health
   - https://biqc-api-dev.azurewebsites.net/api/health
   - https://biqc-web.azurewebsites.net/calibration
   - https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration
Return a concise summary: Is PR merged? Are the PR commits in dev/main? Is Azure dev/prod running images that imply PR code is deployed?
</user_query>
Running the requested checks from the repo directory and collecting outputs.
Re-running commands; previous outputs were empty.

The shell tool in this session completes with exit code 0 but **does not return any stdout/stderr** (even `echo` and `pwd` show nothing, and duration stays at 0 ms), so the requested commands **cannot be executed here in a way that yields their real outputs**.

Below is a **single PowerShell script** you can run locally in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\cyv` that prints **exact labeled outputs** for items 1–10. After you run it, paste the result here if you want the concise summary filled in from real data.

```powershell
Set-Location "C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\cyv"
$sep = "`n---`n"

"=== 1) gh pr view 192 (json) ==="
gh pr view 192 --json number,title,state,mergeStateStatus,reviewDecision,headRefName,baseRefName,isDraft,url
$sep

"=== 2) gh pr diff 192 --name-only ==="
gh pr diff 192 --name-only
$sep

"=== 3) git fetch origin --prune ==="
git fetch origin --prune 2>&1
$sep

"=== 4) git branch -r --contains a91f27d0 ==="
git branch -r --contains a91f27d0
$sep

"=== 5) git branch -r --contains c8a9b65c ==="
git branch -r --contains c8a9b65c
$sep

"=== 6) biqc-api DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME ==="
az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-api --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
$sep

"=== 7) biqc-api-dev DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME ==="
az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-api-dev --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
$sep

"=== 8) biqc-web DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME ==="
az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-web --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
$sep

"=== 9) biqc-web-dev DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME ==="
az webapp config container show -g biqc-production -n biqc-web-dev --query "[?name=='DOCKER_CUSTOM_IMAGE_NAME'].value | [0]" -o tsv
$sep

"=== 10) HTTP status codes ==="
foreach ($u in @(
  "https://biqc-api.azurewebsites.net/api/health",
  "https://biqc-api-dev.azurewebsites.net/api/health",
  "https://biqc-web.azurewebsites.net/calibration",
  "https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration"
)) {
  try {
    $r = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $u -Method Get -MaximumRedirection 0 -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 30
    "$u -> $($r.StatusCode)"
  } catch {
    $resp = $_.Exception.Response
    if ($resp) { "$u -> $([int]$resp.StatusCode)" } else { "$u -> ERROR: $($_.Exception.Message)" }
  }
}
```

### Concise summary (placeholder — needs your real outputs)

| Question | How to decide once you have outputs |
|----------|-------------------------------------|
| **Is PR merged?** | In (1): `state` is `MERGED` (and typically `mergeStateStatus` reflects that). |
| **Are PR commits in dev/main?** | After (3), (4) and (5): if `origin/main` / `origin/dev` (or your repo’s branch names) appear in `--contains` for both SHAs, those commits are reachable from those remotes. |
| **Does Azure imply PR code is deployed?** | Compare (6)–(9) image tags/digests to what your CI publishes for the PR merge commit (or `main`/`dev`). HTTP (10) only shows apps are up, not which image revision is running. |

If you paste the script output (or re-run this in a session where the agent’s terminal returns text), I can answer those three summary bullets **definitively** from the exact JSON, branches, images, and status codes.
